verdict: 2026-06-28 19:38:50 PDT — I would not ship the new shape as-is. The accepted redesigns landed in the right direction: read-only formatter invocations are table-backed, `/close` is generic over backlog kinds, and maturity/risk now use one matrix. The remaining issue is a security invariant that should be explicit before a secret-audit skill writes reports or backlog text. I ran `bash tests/dev_audit_test.sh` successfully; the full gate could not run in this read-only sandbox because `guard_test.sh` needs to create temp repos and `mktemp`/`mkdir` were denied.

findings.title: Secret findings need a redaction invariant before reports are written
findings.claim: The skill deliberately greps for secret patterns and then writes findings to chat, `reviews/audit-<YYYY-MM-DD>.md`, and optionally `BACKLOG.md`, but it never tells Claude to redact matched secret values or scanner raw output. For a repo-audit skill, copying a discovered credential into a permanent report or backlog item compounds the incident and violates the expected handling pattern for secret scanning evidence.
findings.alternative: Add a hard evidence-handling rule: for secret hits, never quote or persist the secret value; report only detector/type, path/location, count, and remediation guidance. Treat raw scanner/grep output as sensitive, summarize it, and keep `BACKLOG.md` items value-free.
findings.win: Prevents the audit artifact from becoming a second secret leak, centralizes a security invariant future audits will copy, and removes a high-impact error path without adding machinery or dependencies.
